skipped.heading: 后果 → 正面
skipped.reason: Positive consequences are restatements of decision rationale; no independent durable knowledge

drafts.title: Main session memory write access creates four distinct failure modes
drafts.compiledTruth: Giving the main session LLM direct memory write access produces four failure modes that corrupt the long-term knowledge base: (1) impulsive writes — LLM writes every interesting observation, polluting memory with ephemeral noise; (2) duplicate writes — repeated sessions recognize the same insight and write it again; (3) cross-source misattribution — project-scoped events land in the wrong source; (4) audit-conversation coupling — writes are entangled with the conversation turn, making post-hoc auditing or replay impossible. Decision: main session is strictly read-only; all writes are delegated exclusively to the sediment sidecar, enabling async vote/dedupe/refine processing and single-provenance audit.

drafts.title: Read-only invariant enforced as Layer 1 mechanic plus Layer 2 best-effort residual
drafts.compiledTruth: The main-session read-only invariant is enforced in two distinct layers. Layer 1 (mechanic): no brain mutation tools are registered on the main session LLM tool surface — write capability is architecturally absent from the callable set. Layer 2 (best-effort residual): LLM can still reach brain storage indirectly via general-purpose tools (bash, edit, write, dispatch_agents). This residual surface is an explicitly accepted tradeoff, not a vulnerability — mitigated by three compensating controls: stdout not returned by default, output redaction, and sediment post-detection audit. The residual is bounded, named, and accepted; it must not be treated as a Layer 1 gap requiring mechanical closure.

drafts.title: prompt_user answers are user-attested signals; Layer 1 read-only mechanic is unaffected
drafts.compiledTruth: prompt_user tool answers originate from physical keyboard input (TUI dialog), making them USER-ATTESTED signals — analogous to Lane A explicit MEMORY: fence blocks authored by the user. This trust classification does NOT soften Layer 1 of the read-only mechanic: main session still cannot directly mutate brain storage, and prompt_user only writes to audit (not to brain entries). Sediment must evaluate any resulting candidate grounded in the complete (question + options + reason + answer) context to prevent the LLM using leading questions to manufacture fake user-attested signals. The sole effect is that sediment curator treats such answer pairs with Lane-A-equivalent confidence, not that main session gains write capability.

drafts.title: Sediment triggers on agent_end and curates autonomously without user action
drafts.compiledTruth: Sediment listens to pi's agent_end event, receiving the complete agent context for that turn. It then autonomously determines: how many independent insights are present; which source each belongs to; whether derived writes are warranted; and whether schema constraints and readback assertions pass. User natural-language write signals ('remember this / I now prefer X / promote this') are extracted directly from the conversation transcript — no dedicated intent tool is required on the main session. This design preserves INV-INVISIBILITY: memory updates without any required user action and without a user-facing approval queue.

drafts.title: Dedicated LLM intent tools for write signaling are an anti-pattern
drafts.compiledTruth: Adding dedicated intent tools (memory_remember / memory_refine / memory_promote) to the main session LLM tool surface so it can signal write intent to sediment is a rejected design. Four reasons: (1) sediment already receives full agent_end context and identifies write intent from conversation text without extra tools; (2) intent tools add main-session cognitive load — when to call them, how to handle failures; (3) creates two competing consumption paths requiring fusion logic and conflict resolution; (4) does not solve the root problem — sediment can still misjudge intent regardless. Revisit only if agent_end context inference is empirically shown to be persistently insufficient after multiple prompt-iteration cycles.
drafts.sourceHeading: 与 v6 原版的差异

drafts.title: Sediment is async: main session must never claim immediate memory persistence
drafts.compiledTruth: Sediment operates asynchronously on agent_end, creating a deliberate gap between a user saying 'remember this' and confirmed brain persistence. Main session LLM must NOT respond with 'I've remembered it / I've stored that' — such a response is a false claim. Correct behavior: acknowledge the request and direct the user to the pending-memory list or sediment log for verification. Compensating control: every agent_end produces a brief log entry recording what was written, what was skipped, and why — a traceable audit trail without requiring any user action.

drafts.title: Brain DB backend must split read and write credentials; main session only sees read role
drafts.compiledTruth: When the brain backend is a database, two access roles must exist: a read role (SELECT only) and a write role (SELECT + INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE). Sediment and the backend service use the write role. The main session environment must only have the read role credentials available. If write credentials are reachable from the main session's bash tool, the LLM can construct a direct INSERT that bypasses sediment, voter, source-router, schema-enforcer, readback, pending queue, and audit log entirely. The config must either contain only read credentials or reside in a sediment-only path the main session cannot access.
drafts.sourceHeading: Guard 1：阻断 direct gbrain CLI write + postgres 直连

drafts.title: Brain audit log, pending queue, and offline fallback paths must be write-blocked from main session
drafts.compiledTruth: Three categories of brain infrastructure paths must be protected from main-session write/edit/bash access, with realpath resolution to defeat symlink attacks. (1) Sediment audit log: contains voter behavior and write-decision provenance; forging it corrupts audit integrity and single-provenance. (2) Sediment pending queue: contains candidate entries; direct injection lets the LLM plant malicious pending entries that sediment later promotes. (3) Offline fallback content: the degraded search source when the live brain is unavailable; poisoning means attacker-controlled content reaches the main session bypassing the entire voter/schema/readback chain. These protection principles apply regardless of the specific storage backend.
drafts.sourceHeading: Guard 2：保护 memory cache / routing marker（v6.5.2 加固）

drafts.title: Dispatched sub-agents must not receive brain write capabilities
drafts.compiledTruth: Main session cannot launder its write prohibition by dispatching a sub-agent that receives bash/edit/write tools — such a sub-agent could write brain storage, bypassing sediment entirely. The enforced rule: sub-agents default to tools=empty. Read-only tools require an explicit allowlist to delegate. Mutating tools are rejected by default or require explicit user confirmation. Sediment voter always runs with tools=empty (never inherits general-purpose tools). This prevents the main session using dispatch_agents as a capability-escalation vector to circumvent the read-only invariant.
